---
title: "Talks and Presentations"
page-layout: full
---

This page lists selected seminars, conference presentations, and research talks.

---

## Invited Seminars and Research Talks

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Computation and Quantum Contextuality

**Bilkent University, Quantum Computing Seminar**  
Ankara, Türkiye  
**17 October 2025**

Invited seminar on quantum contextuality, evolving simplicial-bundle structures, and topology-driven models of computation. The talk discussed how locally compatible data can fail to assemble into a globally consistent account, and how this local-to-global obstruction can motivate computational structures beyond ordinary fixed-background descriptions.

**Links:** [Seminar announcement](https://math.bilkent.edu.tr/seminars/2025-2026/Quantum_17102025.pdf) · [Seminar listing](https://researchseminars.org/seminar/BilkentMathGrad)

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### The Gauge Theoretic Structure of Non-locality and Contextuality

**Neuroscience Modeling and Research Group–SIMMAG**  
İstanbul Technical University, İstanbul, Türkiye  
**21 October 2025**

Invited talk hosted by the Neuroscience Modeling and Research Group–SIMMAG at İstanbul Technical University. The talk presented a gauge-theoretic and bundle-theoretic approach to non-locality and contextuality, focusing on local-to-global obstruction, symmetry, holonomy, and the geometric structure of empirical models.

**Link:** [Invitation letter](files/talks/itu-invitation-letter-imtiyaz.pdf)

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Evolving Networks and Communication Systems

**Leioa Campus / Biofisika context, Spain**  
University of the Basque Country / Biofisika-associated research meeting  
**28 April 2026**

Invited research talk and group discussion on mathematical modelling of evolving networks and communication systems. The talk was connected to network modelling, evolving structures, and mathematical frameworks for complex systems.

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Conference Presentations

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### The Bundle-Theoretic Structure and Computational Modeling of Quantum Contextuality

**Quantum AI and NLP / QNIP 2025**  
Indiana University, Bloomington, USA  
**6–9 August 2025**

Conference presentation connected to the refereed proceedings of the First International Conference on Quantum AI and NLP. The work develops a bundle-theoretic and computational model of quantum contextuality, connecting local-to-global obstruction, contextual semantics, and topology-driven computation.

**Links:** [Conference page](https://qnlp.ai/speakers.html) · [Springer proceedings](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/978-3-032-13883-5)

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### The Bundle-Theoretic Structure and Computational Modeling of Quantum Contextuality

**7th BYMAT Conference — Bringing Young Mathematicians Together**  
University of Seville, Spain  
**17–20 November 2025**

Conference presentation on a geometric and bundle/gauge-theoretic approach to quantum contextuality. The abstract situates the work in relation to sheaf-theoretic contextuality and develops an evolving simplicial-bundle viewpoint involving local-to-global structure, gauge transformations, critical simplices, and virtual loops.

**Link:** [Book of abstracts](https://imus.us.es/congresos/BYMAT/docs/boa.pdf)

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Related Pages

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Research](research.qmd)

Research programme and main research areas.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Publications](publications.qmd)

Peer-reviewed publications, theses, preprints, and public manuscripts.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [CV](cv.qmd)

Academic background, appointments, talks, teaching, supervision, and research activities.

:::
:::
:::

:::
